hypothesis: Incorporating a sequence-level, physics-informed reinforcement learning signal—specifically, using the differentiable TMM-computed ΔE as a reward to fine-tune the decoder after initial cross-entropy pretraining—will reduce the generalization gap between training-paraphrase and held-out validation performance (currently ΔE ≈1 vs. ≈34) by more than 50%, because directly optimizing the spectral color error rather than token-level likelihood will enable the decoder to discover functionally equivalent but structurally distinct layer sequences that satisfy optical targets for unseen material combinations.
